title: Configure an internet-facing ALB with AWS Load Balancer Controller
scenario: The payment service needs an Internet-facing ALB with HTTPS termination. The starter Ingress has two problems: `alb.ingress.kubernetes.io/scheme` is set to `internal` (creating a private load balancer) and the ACM certificate ARN is malformed. Fix both annotations.
starterYaml:
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: payment-ingress
  namespace: default
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/ingress.class: alb
    alb.ingress.kubernetes.io/scheme: internal
    alb.ingress.kubernetes.io/certificate-arn: "invalid-arn"
    alb.ingress.kubernetes.io/listen-ports: '[{"HTTPS":443}]'
spec:
  ingressClassName: alb
  rules:
  - host: payments.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: payment-svc
            port:
              number: 443

hints.text: Two fixes: change `scheme: internal` to `scheme: internet-facing`, and replace `invalid-arn` with a valid ACM certificate ARN.
ALB scheme `internal` creates a load balancer only reachable within the VPC. `internet-facing` creates a public-facing ALB with an Internet-routable DNS name.
ACM certificate ARNs look like `arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc-123`. The region must match where your certificate is issued.

solution:
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: payment-ingress
  namespace: default
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/ingress.class: alb
    alb.ingress.kubernetes.io/scheme: internet-facing
    alb.ingress.kubernetes.io/certificate-arn: "arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc-def-123"
    alb.ingress.kubernetes.io/listen-ports: '[{"HTTPS":443}]'
spec:
  ingressClassName: alb
  rules:
  - host: payments.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: payment-svc
            port:
              number: 443
